properties.scope: Publicly documented European physical FPGA installations and cloud regions exposing FPGA services.

evidence_levels.A: Official operator, provider, institutional or public-infrastructure documentation.
evidence_levels.B: Primary peer-reviewed or equivalent research publication describing the platform.

important_limitations: Presence of FPGA hardware does not demonstrate deployment of medical AI.
Cloud-region coordinates are cartographic label points, not physical data-centre locations.
Counts are reported only when explicitly documented; null means unknown, not zero.
The dataset is a curated pilot and is not an exhaustive census of European FPGA infrastructure.
Availability and lifecycle fields require periodic re-verification.

story_fields.story_order: Cumulative order used by the narrative HTML.
story_fields.medical_ai_status: Separates the documented medical benchmark from infrastructures without public evidence of production medical-AI deployment.
story_fields.map_entity_type: Distinguishes physical sites from cloud-region label points.

properties.name: University of Ferrara – Medical AI FPGA benchmark
properties.facility: Department of Physics and Earth Sciences, Via Saragat 1
properties.fpga_count_note: The medical-image inference benchmark was conducted on an AMD Alveo U55C data-center acceleration card.
properties.memory_note: The U55C used in the benchmark includes 16 GB HBM2.
properties.medical_ai_evidence: Documented research benchmark of a medical-image segmentation workload; this is not presented as a certified production clinical service.
properties.medical_ai_relevance: Origin of the thesis case study and technical reference point for the narrative.
properties.supports_custom_fpga_designs: DPU-based deployment examined in the thesis
properties.evidence_definition: Peer-reviewed primary publication and doctoral research documentation.
properties.coordinate_note: Marker derived from the published University of Ferrara address/map reference; it does not identify a specific server room.
properties.source_title: Benchmarking a DNN for aortic valve calcium lesions segmentation on FPGA-based DPU using the Vitis AI toolchain
properties.thesis_relevance: The benchmark demonstrates execution and measurement of a medical DNN inference workload on an FPGA-based DPU.

properties.name: Makinote FPGA Cluster
properties.access_model: Research platform; access conditions not inferred from the publication
properties.fpga_count_note: 12 nodes across two racks, with 8 Alveo U55C cards per node.
properties.memory_note: Alveo U55C cards include HBM; exact configuration should be checked against the platform paper.
properties.medical_ai_evidence: No public evidence identified here of medical-AI deployment.
properties.medical_ai_relevance: Uses the same Alveo U55C accelerator family examined in the thesis, but for a different research workload.
properties.evidence_definition: Peer-reviewed or primary research publication describing the installed platform.
properties.coordinate_note: Marker placed at the published BSC institutional address, not necessarily at the exact rack room.
properties.source_title: Makinote: An FPGA-Based HW/SW Platform for Pre-Silicon Emulation of RISC-V Designs
properties.thesis_relevance: Demonstrates a large European installation of Alveo U55C cards and provides a geographically concrete comparator for the thesis platform.

properties.name: Noctua 2 FPGA Partition
properties.operator: Paderborn Center for Parallel Computing (PC²), Paderborn University
properties.access_model: Research access, including national NHR programmes and external research proposals
properties.fpga_count_note: 48 Alveo U280 cards and 32 Intel Stratix 10 GX 2800 cards across 36 FPGA nodes.
properties.memory_note: U280 cards include HBM2 and DDR; BittWare cards include DDR, according to PC² system documentation.
properties.medical_ai_evidence: No public evidence identified here of a production medical-AI service.
properties.medical_ai_relevance: A mature, accessible FPGA-rich HPC environment suitable for heterogeneous-computing experiments.
properties.evidence_definition: Official system and access documentation from the operating centre.
properties.coordinate_note: Approximate marker for PC² Building X at Mersinweg 5.
properties.thesis_relevance: Shows that European national HPC services can expose sizeable FPGA partitions through structured access programmes.

properties.name: MeluXina – FPGA Accelerator Module
properties.access_model: EuroHPC research and industrial access programmes
properties.fpga_count_note: 20 CPU–FPGA hybrid nodes, each with 2 BittWare 520N-MX cards.
properties.memory_note: Each FPGA card is documented with 16 GB HBM.
properties.medical_ai_evidence: No public evidence identified here of a production medical-AI service on this FPGA partition.
properties.medical_ai_relevance: Directly relevant as an operational European HPC infrastructure with a documented FPGA partition for AI workloads.
properties.coordinate_note: Approximate marker for the published Bissen DC2 address; not a surveyed rack coordinate.
properties.source_title: EuroHPC Technical Guidelines and LuxProvide/LuxConnect facility documentation
properties.thesis_relevance: Example of a controlled European HPC environment in which FPGA-accelerated AI inference could be deployed and benchmarked.

properties.name: AWS Europe (Frankfurt) – EC2 F2
properties.fpga_count_note: Physical fleet size is not publicly stated; an F2 instance can expose up to 8 FPGAs.
properties.memory_note: 16 GB HBM per FPGA is documented by AWS.
properties.medical_ai_evidence: No claim is made that medical-AI workloads are currently deployed in this region.
properties.medical_ai_relevance: Commercially accessible European FPGA capacity that could support controlled inference experiments subject to governance and certification requirements.
properties.evidence_definition: Official cloud-provider availability and product documentation.
properties.coordinate_note: Frankfurt city centroid used only as a cartographic label for the AWS region; it is not a data-centre coordinate.
properties.source_title: Amazon EC2 F2 regional availability and product page
properties.thesis_relevance: Represents FPGA as an on-demand service rather than a locally owned accelerator.

properties.name: AWS Europe (London) – EC2 F2
properties.fpga_count_note: Physical fleet size is not publicly stated; an F2 instance can expose up to 8 FPGAs.
properties.memory_note: 16 GB HBM per FPGA is documented by AWS.
properties.medical_ai_evidence: No claim is made that medical-AI workloads are currently deployed in this region.
properties.medical_ai_relevance: Commercially accessible FPGA capacity in a European cloud region.
properties.evidence_definition: Official cloud-provider availability and product documentation.
properties.coordinate_note: London city centroid used only as a cartographic label for the AWS region; it is not a data-centre coordinate.
properties.source_title: Amazon EC2 F2 regional availability and product page
properties.thesis_relevance: Represents FPGA as an on-demand service rather than a locally owned accelerator.

properties.name: Microsoft Azure West Europe – NP-series
properties.access_model: Commercial cloud service; new reserved purchases ended on 2026-04-02
properties.fpga_count_note: Physical fleet size is not publicly stated; NP VM sizes expose 1 to 4 FPGAs.
properties.memory_note: Microsoft documentation describes the NP family as U250-based FPGA-accelerated VMs.
properties.medical_ai_evidence: No claim is made that medical-AI workloads are currently deployed in this region.
properties.medical_ai_relevance: A documented European cloud FPGA service whose planned retirement illustrates the need for temporal fields in the GIS.
properties.evidence_definition: Official cloud-provider product, availability and retirement documentation.
properties.coordinate_note: Netherlands centroid used because Microsoft publishes the region location, not individual data-centre coordinates.
properties.thesis_relevance: Shows both the availability and lifecycle risk of cloud-hosted FPGA acceleration.
